justification: All pass criteria met: prototype pollution identified by name, __proto__ attack vector explained with specific payload ({"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}}), global Object.prototype impact explained ("polluting every object in the process"), safe merge fixes recommended (guard keys, Object.hasOwn, schema validation, well-tested library), and safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Exact attack payload provided: {"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}} with clear explanation of how target["__proto__"] resolves to Object.prototype
Explains global process-wide impact: ({}).isAdmin === true after a single malicious request
Also catches constructor.prototype as an additional pollution vector
Multiple fix strategies offered: key guarding, Object.hasOwn, schema validation (zod), well-tested libraries (lodash.mergeWith, deepmerge)
Goes beyond rubric requirements by identifying two additional critical/high findings: falsy-value bug and mass-assignment vulnerability
Production readiness section notes that monitoring would not detect the attack and suggests WAF rules for __proto__ in JSON bodies
Rollback plan correctly notes that polluted prototypes persist until process restart, not just code revert
